scores.materiality_reasoning: Jury service involves legal power and direct financial pay (sixpence).
scores.generality_reasoning: Access to public office/judicial bodies is a broad civic category.
scores.persistence_reasoning: Service is recurring/episodic based on court summons throughout life.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Being 'Athenian' refers to citizenship, a durable legal birth-status.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Lucian describes the established Athenian judicial system of his era.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Verbatim cites the rule as a formal proclamation of standard procedure.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Jury service is a legal right, not part of the definition of 'Athenian'.

scores.materiality_reasoning: Taxation is a direct material transfer of money/wealth.
scores.generality_reasoning: Tax obligation is a broad, structural fiscal category.
scores.persistence_reasoning: Tax liability persists as long as the status of residency holds.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Naturalized foreigner status (metic) is a durable social identity.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Refers to the real tax laws for metics in Lucian's cultural context.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Reports tax-paying as a matter-of-fact legal obligation.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Paying taxes is a legal duty, not the definition of a foreigner.

scores.materiality_reasoning: Right of appeal has direct material effects on legal/property outcomes.
scores.generality_reasoning: A specific but recurring legal procedural right.
scores.persistence_reasoning: The right applies to a single, discrete legal trial context.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Addresses 'Athenians' as citizens, a structural birth-status.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Describes contemporary legal avenues of appeal in Athenian courts.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Presented as a formal rule within a satirical court setting.
scores.tautology_reasoning: The right to appeal is a procedural law, not intrinsic to citizenship.

scores.materiality_reasoning: Direct payment of money (sixpence) for services rendered.
scores.generality_reasoning: Specific financial compensation for a particular civic role.
scores.persistence_reasoning: Attached to a recurring role performed throughout life.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Citizenship is a hard-to-change legal birth-status.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Refers to the actual misthos (jury pay) system in Athens.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: States the amount and condition of pay as a functional rule.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Receiving pay is not part of the definition of 'Athenian'.

scores.materiality_reasoning: Freedom of movement and lifestyle choice is a foundational material freedom.
scores.generality_reasoning: Covers a foundational axis of legal personhood and autonomy.
scores.persistence_reasoning: Structural right inherent in lifelong citizenship status.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Citizenship is a legal status fixed by birth or law.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Discusses the rights of citizens in contemporary free states.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Argued as a philosophical point but based on legal standing.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Autonomy in lifestyle is not the same as the definition of citizen.

scores.materiality_reasoning: Cash payment for jury service is a tangible material resource.
scores.generality_reasoning: Tied to a specific administrative condition (earning the fee).
scores.persistence_reasoning: Concerns a momentary payoff for a single completed case.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Group is 'jurors' for a specific suspended trial; episodic role.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Reflects standard administrative practices regarding public fees.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Justice reports the withholding of pay due to non-completion of work.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Getting paid for a specific case is not the definition of a juror.

scores.materiality_reasoning: Right to trial location determines legal costs and protections.
scores.generality_reasoning: A recurring jurisdictional rule regarding legal venue.
scores.persistence_reasoning: Applies for the duration of a single legal procedure.
scores.immutability_reasoning: 'Outsiders' is defined by birth/ethnicity outside the polis.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Reflects real contemporary jurisdictional limits in Athens.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Justice asserts standard jurisdictional boundaries as fact.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Exclusion from a specific court is not the definition of 'foreigner'.

scores.materiality_reasoning: Voting in the Ekklesia grants direct political power.
scores.generality_reasoning: Political power is a foundational societal axis.
scores.persistence_reasoning: Voters retain this right as a lifelong structural feature of citizenship.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Citizenship is a durable, legal birth-status.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Describes the active functioning of the Athenian assembly.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Verbatim addresses the group as active political participants.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Voting is a shared right, not the definition of being Athenian.

scores.materiality_reasoning: Eligibility to serve on a jury is access to a civic role.
scores.generality_reasoning: Specific but recurring access to the judicial system.
scores.persistence_reasoning: Eligibility is a status that lasts for a specific life stage.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Citizenship is a hard-to-change legal birth-status.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Reflects the actual composition of Athenian law courts.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Describes professional compensation as a simple fact of the trial.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Juror eligibility is a right, not the definition of a citizen.

scores.materiality_reasoning: Retaining a dowry (property/wealth) has significant material consequences.
scores.generality_reasoning: A major category of property and marital law.
scores.persistence_reasoning: The outcome of a divorce procedure determines property for a lifetime.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Sex/gender is a strictly immutable biological/caste category.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Refers to standard contemporary Athenian marital and divorce laws.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Mentions divorce courts as an existing factual legal institution.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Being female does not include the definition of having a dowry.

scores.materiality_reasoning: Financial fees for service are tangible wealth resources.
scores.generality_reasoning: Standard compensation for a recurring public service.
scores.persistence_reasoning: Recurring annual/cycle resource based on civic duty.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Refers to citizens acting as jurors; structural legal status.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Describes contemporary juror pay in Athens.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Verbatim states the fee as a direct promise within procedure.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Receiving a fee is a right, not the definition of citizenship.

scores.materiality_reasoning: A professional title is a status/recognition resource with weak material payoff.
scores.generality_reasoning: Narrowly scoped to social address within an occupation.
scores.persistence_reasoning: Titles usually persist through a vocational career.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Occupation is a durable social identity requiring training.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Reflects contemporary social norms of address and recognition.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Argued as a point of etiquette but based on common usage.
scores.tautology_reasoning: A title is a social right, not the definition of the artisan themselves.

scores.materiality_reasoning: Profit from labor is direct financial subsistence and wealth.
scores.generality_reasoning: Covers the broad class of economic productivity and gain.
scores.persistence_reasoning: Expectation of self-sustenance is structural across the work-life.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Free status is a hard-to-change legal birth-status.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Reflects contemporary socio-economic expectations of the Roman era.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Reports a general socio-economic expectation as a fact.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Working for profit is not part of the definition of 'free man'.

scores.materiality_reasoning: Peculium or earnings constitute tangible material property.
scores.generality_reasoning: Covers the general category of income for a large social class.
scores.persistence_reasoning: Labor skill and associated earnings persist for the term of slavery.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Enslavement is a durable legal/structural birth-status.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Reflects the common practice of skilled slave labor and peculium.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Acknowledges slave labor as a common observed economic fact.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Labor utility is not the definition of being a slave.

scores.materiality_reasoning: Abtract 'pleasure' or 'satisfying desires' is a non-material state.
scores.generality_reasoning: Relates to a broad class of foundational life outcomes.
scores.persistence_reasoning: General lifestyle outcome that holds as long as wealth status holds.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Wealth bracket is a changeable but durable social identity.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Discusses a sociological reality contemporary to the author.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Heavy rhetorical framing within a philosophical dialogue.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Pleasure is the result of wealth, not the definition of the poor.

scores.materiality_reasoning: Protection from bodily harm (flogging) is a material entitlement.
scores.generality_reasoning: Recurring protection from corporal punishment in a specific setting.
scores.persistence_reasoning: Bounded to the life stage/period spent in education.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Role-bound identity (student) during a specific phase of life.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Describes real corporal punishment practices in schools of the time.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Reports school floggings as an observed, standard reality.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Being a student does not mean being flogged by definition.

scores.materiality_reasoning: Mandated holidays are a right to rest with material effects on labor.
scores.generality_reasoning: Covers a broad category of labor rights and social rest.
scores.persistence_reasoning: Standard labor practice holding across a long-term career.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Artisan status is an identity requiring training/guild membership.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Reflects the actual holiday/work calendar of ancient labor.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Reports the holiday frequency of various professions as common knowledge.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Restricted rest is not part of the definition of an artisan.

scores.materiality_reasoning: Food is a basic material means of subsistence.
scores.generality_reasoning: Tied to a hyper-specific performance metric in one setting.
scores.persistence_reasoning: Momentary/one-off reward for a single academic exercise.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Role-bound identity limited to students in a school setting.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Describes a plausible contemporary pedagogical practice.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Reports specific disciplinary habits as factual occurrences.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Access to food is a reward, not the definition of a student.

scores.materiality_reasoning: Access to the royal table is membership in an elite body with material sustenance.
scores.generality_reasoning: Narrowly scoped to a single royal household institution.
scores.persistence_reasoning: Describes a privilege held 'all the days of his life'.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Identity of a favorite or 'parasite' is a durable social status.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Discusses Homeric/Heroic legendary paradigms.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Interpretive claim about ancient epic literature.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Royal dining is an acquired right, not the definition of the group.

scores.materiality_reasoning: Exemption from service affects bodily integrity and risk of death.
scores.generality_reasoning: Mobilization is a broad, structural category of state obligation.
scores.persistence_reasoning: Bounded specifically to the years of military age.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Based on age, which is strictly biological at a given moment.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Describes a standard Greek/Roman call to colors for adult men.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Verbatim cites the commander's summons to the colors as fact.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Service is a legal obligation, not the definition of being a certain age.
